audit_result.proof_trace: Line 148 uses blob_entry.epoch (the schedule entry's activation epoch) instead of the current epoch parameter when computing the blob params hash. This makes fork digest constant across all epochs within the same blob schedule period, violating the epoch-dependent property. Nodes with different blob schedule activation epochs but identical current blob limits will compute different digests despite having identical network state.
audit_result.attack_scenario: Attacker deploys nodes with blob schedule configured at different activation epochs (e.g., epoch 100 vs 200) but same max_blobs value. At epoch 300, both node sets are in identical state but compute different fork digests (hash(100||15) vs hash(200||15)). This causes P2P topic subscription mismatch and network partition between otherwise-compatible nodes.

PROP-56ad1eb2-inv-005.text: Fork digest must incorporate blob parameters; mismatched digests cause network partition.
PROP-56ad1eb2-inv-005.assertion: forall epoch e: fork_digest(e) == trunc4(fork_data_root(e) XOR hash(blob_params(e)))
